summary: notify-funcs fetches and executes remote code on require via Function constructor
details: On require, index.js performs an HTTP GET to http://vercel-five-coral.vercel.app/defy/v3, takes the response's `token` field, constructs a function via `new (Function.constructor)('require', res.token)`, and invokes it with the installer's `require` in scope. This gives the remote endpoint arbitrary code execution inside any Node process that imports the package. The package presents itself as a fast JSON logger and ships a pino-like `lib/` tree (module.exports.pino, lib/proto.js, lib/levels.js, lib/redaction.js) as a cover story to attract installers expecting pino-compatible behavior. The indirect Function-constructor pattern combined with a plain-HTTP, mutable remote payload is a deliberate evasion of static pattern-matching. The remote host is attacker-mutable, so the executed code can change at any time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
